title: Frontend uses backend-provided public download tokens for /d/{token} links

requirements.summary: Ensure public download links are always built and displayed using backend-provided file.publicToken and use the /d/{token} format (no client-side token generation).
acceptanceCriteria: The "Copy Link" action continues to copy URLs in the format `${origin}/d/${file.publicToken}` (as built by frontend/src/utils/links.ts).
After uploading a new file, the token shown/used in the copied link is the new random token returned in the file list metadata (not a numeric string).
Visiting a copied link routes to the existing public download page and attempts download using the token from the URL.

file_operations.description: Harden buildPublicDownloadUrl(token) to strictly construct `${origin}/d/${token}` using the backend-provided token (no generation), including safe encoding of the token for URL usage while preserving the /d/{token} format.

Ensure the "Copy Link" UI always uses `file.publicToken` from backend metadata (never `file.id`), and add a small guard to disable/hide copying when `publicToken` is missing/empty with English user-facing messaging.

Ensure admin "Copy Link" actions continue to use `file.publicToken` from backend metadata (never numeric IDs), and mirror the same missing/empty-token guard behavior as the user table for consistent UX.

Normalize the token read from the `/d/$token` route param (e.g., decode/trim) before invoking the existing download mutation, and keep routing behavior unchanged so copied links open the existing public download page and attempt download using the URL token.
